annotations.label: Klein bottle (one-sided surface)

anchor.snippet: is an example of a surface with no distinct inside or outside

annotations.note: Mathlib defines no Klein bottle or surfaces; the only "Klein" decls are the unrelated Klein four-group.

annotations.label: Boundaryless, not embeddable in 3-space

anchor.snippet: the Klein bottle is boundaryless, like a sphere or torus

annotations.note: No Klein bottle exists in Mathlib, so its boundarylessness and non-embeddability in R³ are not stated.

annotations.label: Fundamental polygon of the Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: is a fundamental polygon of the Klein bottle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no fundamental-polygon/edge-identification construction for the Klein bottle.

annotations.label: Immersion in three-dimensional space

anchor.snippet: this is thus an immersion of the Klein bottle in the three-dimensional space

annotations.note: A generic immersion notion exists in Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/Immersion.lean but no Klein bottle immersion into R³ is constructed.

annotations.label: No boundary and non-orientable

anchor.snippet: the Klein bottle has no boundary

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Klein bottle and no general manifold non-orientability theory to state this.

annotations.label: Proper bottle does not self-intersect

anchor.snippet: The Klein bottle, proper, does not self-intersect

annotations.note: No Klein bottle object exists, so claims about its self-intersection are absent.

annotations.label: Quotient space of the square

anchor.snippet: the Klein bottle is the quotient space described as the square

annotations.note: Mathlib has general quotient-topology machinery but no Klein bottle defined as a quotient of the unit square.

annotations.label: Non-orientable closed manifold, embeds in R4

anchor.snippet: the Klein bottle is a two-dimensional manifold which is not orientable

annotations.note: Neither the Klein bottle as a manifold nor its embedding into R⁴ is formalized.

annotations.label: Spherinder Klein bottle generalization

anchor.snippet: creating a 3-manifold which cannot be embedded in R 4 but can be in R 5

annotations.note: This higher-dimensional spherinder generalization is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fiber bundle over the circle

anchor.snippet: The Klein bottle can be seen as a fiber bundle over the circle

annotations.note: Generic FiberBundle infrastructure exists (Mathlib/Topology/FiberBundle/Basic.lean) but the Klein bottle as a circle bundle is not constructed.

annotations.label: Joining two Möbius strips

anchor.snippet: by joining the edges of two Möbius strips

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Möbius strip; "Möbius" only refers to the number-theoretic arithmetic function.

annotations.label: CW structure, Euler characteristic and homology groups

anchor.snippet: the Klein bottle can be given a CW complex structure

annotations.note: Generic CWComplex and SingularHomology infrastructure exist, but the Klein bottle's specific CW structure, Euler characteristic, and homology groups are not computed.

annotations.label: 2-1 covering map from the torus

anchor.snippet: There is a 2-1 covering map from the torus to the Klein bottle

annotations.note: Mathlib defines IsCoveringMap generally but has no torus-to-Klein-bottle double cover.

annotations.label: Fundamental group presentation

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of the Klein bottle can be determined as the group of deck transformations

annotations.note: FundamentalGroup is defined generically, but the Klein bottle's group presentation ⟨a,b | abab⁻¹⟩ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Six colors suffice (Heawood exception)

anchor.snippet: Six colors suffice to color any map on the surface of a Klein bottle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no map-coloring/Heawood theory for surfaces.

annotations.label: Connected sum of two projective planes

anchor.snippet: A Klein bottle is homeomorphic to the connected sum of two projective planes

annotations.note: Mathlib has no connected-sum operation and no real projective plane RP² as a surface.

annotations.label: One-sided when embedded in Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: When embedded in Euclidean space, the Klein bottle is one-sided

annotations.note: One-sidedness of the Klein bottle in Euclidean space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dissection into two mirror-image Möbius strips

anchor.snippet: Dissecting a Klein bottle into halves along its plane of symmetry results in two mirror image Möbius strips

annotations.note: No Klein bottle or Möbius strip exists in Mathlib to express this dissection.

annotations.label: Homology classes of simple-closed curves

anchor.snippet: the only homology classes which contain simple-closed curves are as follows

annotations.note: This classification of simple-closed-curve homology classes on the Klein bottle is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Figure-8 immersion

anchor.snippet: one can start with a Möbius strip and curl it to bring the edge to the midline

annotations.note: The figure-8 immersion construction of the Klein bottle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cross section is a 2:1 Lissajous curve

anchor.snippet: the cross section is a 2:1 Lissajous curve

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Lissajous curves and no Klein bottle cross-section parametrization.

annotations.label: Non-intersecting 4-D parametrization

anchor.snippet: A non-intersecting 4-D parametrization can be modeled after that of the flat torus

annotations.note: No explicit non-self-intersecting R⁴ parametrization of the Klein bottle exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pinched torus / Möbius tube parametrization

anchor.snippet: The pinched torus is perhaps the simplest parametrization of the Klein bottle

annotations.note: The pinched-torus / Möbius-tube parametrization of the Klein bottle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bottle-shape 3D immersion parametrization

anchor.snippet: The following parametrization of the usual 3-dimensional immersion of the bottle itself is much more complicated

annotations.note: The explicit bottle-shaped 3D immersion parametrization is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three regular homotopy classes

anchor.snippet: Regular 3D immersions of the Klein bottle fall into three regular homotopy classes

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of regular homotopy or its classes of immersions.

annotations.label: Traditional achiral, figure-8 chiral

anchor.snippet: The traditional Klein bottle immersion is achiral

annotations.note: Chirality of Klein bottle immersions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cutting yields Möbius strips of opposite chirality

anchor.snippet: it breaks into two Möbius strips of opposite chirality

annotations.note: Neither Möbius strips nor their chirality after cutting the Klein bottle appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solid Klein bottle as Möbius strip times interval

anchor.snippet: a solid Klein bottle is homeomorphic to the Cartesian product of a Möbius strip and a closed interval

annotations.note: Mathlib has no solid Klein bottle or Möbius strip, so this homeomorphism is not stated.
